signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.494 — AUD outperformance hits critical thresholds as the G8 leader, supported by a +360bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75%). JPY remains the G8 laggard despite normal volatility (VIX 17.9), as carry-seeking flows dominate safe-haven demand. Retail sentiment is 87% short, diverging from the institutional trend and increasing the risk of a short-squeeze if the 115.00 level is tested.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Fed Bias
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.917 — Boston Fed's Collins raises the prospect of rate hikes, supporting the +287bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. However, the options market shows a strong directional bias for JPY strength with 25d RR at -1.62, signaling institutional hedging against intervention or a hawkish BoJ shift. COT data shows a crowded JPY net short of -61,340, leaving the pair vulnerable to rapid deleveraging if 160.00 is approached.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5932 — COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) shows speculators are heavily positioned against the NZD despite a hawkish market bias for the next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 27 May. This positioning extreme (long% < 20%) creates a high-conviction short-squeeze setup if the RBNZ signals a hike. Retail is 71% long, aligning with the squeeze but contradicting the institutional short position.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility + Sentiment + UK Data
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3525 — Options market pricing reflects elevated fear with a +4.2% Vol Risk Premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.8%). UK house prices falling at the fastest pace since 2023 and Iran-related geopolitical tensions are driving hedging demand. While carry is neutral (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the moderate put skew (RR -0.96) suggests the market is paying for downside protection ahead of UK GDP data.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Extreme + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7817 — Volatility risk premium is at an extreme +11.3% (IV 18.6% vs HV30 7.3%), signaling massive demand for options protection despite a neutral 25d RR (-0.06). The +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) structurally supports the USD, but the vol spike suggests the market is bracing for a regime shift or safe-haven CHF bid. Watch for VIX > 25 to trigger carry unwind.
